items.q: Why is energy lost between trophic levels?
items.correct: Energy is lost mainly through respiration (as heat), movement and undigested or excreted material, so less passes on.
items.misconception: Energy is destroyed as it moves up a food chain.
items.random_wrong: Energy is lost because predators eat only a few prey.
items.why: Students think energy disappears rather than being transferred to the surroundings, breaking conservation of energy.

items.correct: Populations become reproductively isolated and accumulate genetic differences until they can no longer interbreed to produce fertile offspring.
items.misconception: A new species forms the moment one individual gains a useful mutation.
items.random_wrong: New species form whenever two different animals mate.
items.why: Students think speciation happens suddenly in one individual rather than through gradual isolation of populations.

items.q: Why does more atmospheric CO2 warm the Earth?
items.correct: CO2 absorbs outgoing infrared (long-wave) radiation and re-emits some back toward the surface, trapping heat.
items.misconception: CO2 warms the Earth by making a hole in the ozone layer.
items.random_wrong: CO2 warms the Earth by reflecting extra sunlight onto the ground.
items.why: Students confuse the greenhouse effect with ozone depletion.

items.q: Net primary productivity equals gross primary productivity minus:
items.correct: energy lost in respiration by producers
items.why: Correct answer: energy lost in respiration by producers. “energy gained from consumers” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Bare rock colonised first by lichens, then mosses, then larger plants is an example of:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: succession. “eutrophication” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: New species can form when populations become:
items.why: Correct answer: reproductively isolated. “larger in number” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Rising atmospheric CO2 increases warming because CO2:
items.misconception: reflects sunlight back to the surface
items.why: Correct answer: absorbs and re-emits infrared (long-wave) radiation. “reflects sunlight back to the surface” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If GPP is 12000 kJ m^-2 yr^-1 and respiratory loss by producers is 4000 kJ m^-2 yr^-1, what is the NPP?
items.why: The real figure is 8000 kJ m^-2 yr^-1. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
